messages.content: average reaction time for young males

As an AI language model, I don't have access to current data. However, according to a study published in the journal "PLOS ONE" in 2014, the average reaction time for young adult males was 250 milliseconds (ms). However, this can vary depending on age, physical fitness, and other factors.

﻿
Which of the following forms must an employer file for each employee
showing taxes were withheld?
A. W-2
B. 1-9
C. W-4

﻿
Which of the following best describes the concept of "fixed" expenses?
A. Expenses that stay the same each month and do not change.
B. Expenses made to take care of your loved
ones.
C. A payment that you make each month that may vary in price (gasoline).
D. An expense you will need to pay for your entire life regardless of income.
A. Expenses that stay the same each month and do not change.

﻿
If you are paying for a home, the home will be an ASSET if what is true about it?
A. The house is worth more than what you owe on the home loan.
B. The monthly payments are small.
C. The house is not worth the total amount of the loan owed.
D. The monthly payments are large.
A. The house is worth more than what you owe on the home loan.

﻿
Large businesses usually work with what kind of banking institution for their financial accounts?
A. credit unions
B. brokerage firms
C. savings and loan banks
D. commercial banks
D. Commercial banks.

﻿
Which of the following best describes the FDIC?
A. It is a state bank.
B. It is the organization that prints the money.
C. It is the organization that creates the coins.
D. It is a government insurance agency.
D. It is a government insurance agency.

﻿
If you don't want to pay an ATM fee, it is best to use the ATM located where?
A. At any bank in that nation.
B. At a local gas station that has an ATM.
C. At the bank or branch where the checking account is located.
D. In a large shopping mall where purchases are made.
C. At the bank or branch where the checking account is located.

﻿
Which of the following is true about purchasing insurance?
A. It is illegal in some states.
B. It involves financial risk.
C. It is risk free.
D. It is always inexpensive.
B. It involves financial risk.

﻿
Which insurance offers a list of providers that their members MUST choose from or they will not be covered?
A. HSA
B. PPO
C. DAI
D. HMO
D. HMO.

﻿
The purpose of automotive insurance is mainly to protect who?
A. The officers responding to the call.
B. The car dealer who sells you the car.
C. The person who caused the accident and has damages.
D. The person who did not cause the accident but has damages.
D. The person who did not cause the accident but has damages.

﻿
Which of the options below are NOT commonly a part of renter's insurance?
A. Coverage for the renter's possessions against loss, damage, or theft.
B. Coverage for the physical structure of the building.
C. Coverage against bodily injury resulting from something in your apartment (i.e. injury from a dog).
B. Coverage for the physical structure of the building.
